'''Paul McShane''' (born 19 November 1989) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} or {{rlp|sh}} for the [[York Knights]] in the [[Super League]]. He has played for [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level, and was the recipient of the [[Man of Steel award]] in 2020.

McShane began his career with the [[Leeds Rhinos]], where he progressed through the academy and played for five seasons, winning the [[2012 World Club Challenge]]. During this time he also featured on loan for [[Hull FC]], the [[Widnes Vikings]] and the [[Hunslet Hawks]]. He signed for the [[Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in 2014 and spent two years there. Partway through the 2015 season, McShane joined the [[Castleford Tigers]], where he would go on to spend the majority of his career. Across a 10-year stay, he made 223 appearances and scored 31 tries, 69 goals and 2 drop goals. He played in the [[2017 Super League Grand Final|2017 Grand Final]] and [[2021 Challenge Cup final]], won the 2017 [[League Leaders' Shield]], and was appointed Castleford [[Captain (sports)|club captain]] from 2022 until his departure at the end of 2024. He joined the [[York Knights]] ahead of 2025.

==Background==
McShane was born in Belle Isle,[[Leeds]], West Yorkshire, England.

McShane was captain of the [[Merlyn Rees High School]] team. He joined the [[Leeds Rhinos]]' academy from [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]] in 2006. He was named Junior Academy Player of the Year in 2007<ref>{{Cite web |last=Daly |first=Phil |date=2007-10-08 |title=ACADEMY AWARD WINNERS REVEALED |url=https://www.therhinos.co.uk/2007/10/09/academy-award-winners-revealed/ |access-date=2019-06-15 |website=Leeds Rhinos |language=en}}</ref> and kicked five goals in their Grand Final win over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].

McShane was named in the England U18 Academy squad to tour Australia in June 2008.

==Club career==
===Leeds Rhinos===
McShane signed a five-year contract with Leeds in July 2008, keeping him at the club until at least 2013.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/leeds/7511892.stm|title=Leeds secure four academy stars|date=2008-07-17|access-date=2019-06-15|language=en-GB}}</ref> 
[[File:Paul McShane Leeds Rhinos.jpg|thumb|right|McShane coming off the field for the Leeds Rhinos in [[Florida]] in 2009]]
He made his first team debut on the opening day of the 2009 Super League season against [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7866936.stm|title=Leeds 28–6 Celtic Crusaders|date=2009-02-06|access-date=2019-06-15|language=en-GB}}</ref> He made two further appearances in 2009, including his first start against [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref name="RLP" />

McShane was a replacement in the Leeds side that won the 2012 [[World Club Challenge]], beating [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly-Warringah]] 26–12.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/17031266|title=Leeds Rhinos 26–12 Manly Sea Eagles|date=2012-02-17|access-date=2019-06-15|language=en-GB}}</ref>
[[File:Paul McShane Leeds.jpg|thumb|right|McShane playing for the Leeds Rhinos in 2013]]

In 2013, McShane made 23 appearances for Leeds. However, at the end of the season, [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] agreed to release McShane from his contract and placed him on the transfer list.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2013-11-02 |title=Paul McShane: Leeds Rhinos agree to transfer list hooker |language=en-GB |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24787420 |access-date=2019-06-15}}</ref>

====Hull F.C. (loan)====
In April 2010, McShane signed for [[Hull F.C.]] on a one-month loan deal.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-sign-rhino-mcshane-on-loan/|title=Hull sign Rhino McShane on loan|last=|date=2010-04-21|website=Love Rugby League|language=en-GB|access-date=2019-06-15}}</ref> He made 4 appearances for the club.<ref name="RLP" /> McShane made a further 9 Super League appearances for Leeds in 2010. He also scored his first try, against [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/8729671.stm|title=Harlequins 22–42 Leeds|date=2010-06-12|access-date=2019-06-15|language=en-GB}}</ref>
[[File:Paul McShane.JPG|thumb|right|McShane playing for Hull FC in 2010]]

====Widnes Vikings (loan)====
In May 2012, Widnes Vikings signed McShane on a loan deal for the rest of the season.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/18277977|title=Widnes Vikings take Leeds Rhinos hooker Paul McShane on loan|date=2012-05-31|access-date=2019-06-15|language=en-GB}}</ref> McShane made 11 appearances during this period, scoring 3 tries and kicking 4 goals.<ref name="RLRKC" />

====Hunslet Hawks (DR)====
McShane played for the [[Hunslet Hawks]] in the [[RFL Championship]] during 2013, through their [[RFL dual registration|dual registration]] arrangement with Leeds.<ref>{{Cite web |date=24 July 2013 |title=McShane to join up with Hunslet |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/mcshane-to-join-up-with-hunslet |access-date=9 October 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity Wildcats===
McShane was signed by [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in November 2013.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/25128727|title=Wakefield Wildcats sign Paul McShane from Leeds Rhinos|date=2013-11-28|access-date=2019-06-15|language=en-GB}}</ref>
[[File:Paul McShane Wakefield.JPG|thumb|right|McShane playing for Wakefield in December 2014]]

===Castleford Tigers===
In July 2015, McShane joined [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] on a two-and-a-half-year contract as part of a deal that saw [[Scott Moore (rugby league)|Scott Moore]] move the other way on loan.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=4274|title=Hookers trade places|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2019-06-15}}</ref> He made his club debut against [[Hull F.C.]] in the first game of the Super 8s.

McShane was ever-present for [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in the [[2016 Castleford Tigers season|2016 season]], appearing in every game.<ref name="RLP" /> He was an integral part of the [[2017 Castleford Tigers season|2017 squad]] that won the [[League Leaders' Shield]] and he played in the [[2017 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/41536394|title=Grand Final 2017: Castleford 6–24 Leeds Rhinos|date=7 October 2017|work=BBC Sport|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref> He was named Castleford Tigers' 3rd Place 2017 Player of the Year.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=5649|title=2017 Winners|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2019-06-15}}</ref>

In January 2018, it was announced that McShane had signed a new five-year deal with the Tigers.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2018-01-09|title=Paul McShane: Castleford Tigers hooker signs new five-year deal|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/42621954|access-date=2022-02-03|website=BBC Sport}}</ref> McShane had an outstanding personal season for Castleford in [[2018 Castleford Tigers season|2018]], branded "unlucky" not to be nominated for the Man of Steel award by two-time winner [[Paul Sculthorpe]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/45671850|title=Steve Prescott Man of Steel: Ben Barba, James Roby and John Bateman are nominated for the award|date=27 September 2018|access-date=1 February 2026|website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He dominated the club's end-of-season awards, being recognised as the Fans' Player of the Year, Directors' Player of the Year, Players' Player of the Year, and 1st Place Player of the Year.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-hooker-mcshane-rakes-up-full-set-of-end-of-year-club-awards-247921|title=Castleford Tigers: Hooker McShane rakes up full set of end-of-year club awards|date=1 October 2018|access-date=1 February 2026|website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=6122|title=2018 Awards Winners|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2019-06-15}}</ref>

Despite disruption due to injuries within Castleford's squad and the [[Impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on rugby league|COVID-19 pandemic]], McShane appeared in every game in [[2020 Castleford Tigers season|2020]]. In November, McShane was voted Super League's 2020 [[Man of Steel Awards|Man of Steel]] – Castleford head coach [[Daryl Powell]] said, "He is one of the best nines in the game bar none and his all-round game is fantastic."<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/1790/season-review-castleford-tigers/|title=Season Review Castleford|website=www.superleague.co.uk}}</ref><ref name="Sky MOS">{{Cite web|last=Bazeley|first=Marc|date=2020-11-24|title=Man of Steel: How Paul McShane beat the odds to claim Super League accolade|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12198/12140844/man-of-steel-how-paul-mcshane-beat-the-odds-to-claim-super-league-accolade|access-date=2022-02-02|website=Sky Sports}}</ref> He became the first player since 2011 to win the award with his team failing to qualify for the play-offs.<ref name="Sky MOS"/>

McShane signed a new three-year deal with Castleford in July 2021, extending his contract to the end of 2025.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-07-02|title=Paul McShane: Castleford hooker signs new three-year deal with club|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57700830|access-date=2022-02-03|website=BBC Sport}}</ref> On 17 July 2021, he played for Castleford in their [[2021 Challenge Cup final]] loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57828390|title=Challenge Cup: St Helens beat Castleford 26–12 for first Wembley victory since 2008|work=BBC Sport }}</ref> In recognition of his continued outstanding form, McShane was named Directors' Player of the Year and won the Immortals Award, voted for by Castleford legends, at the club's 2021 end-of-season awards.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-10-04|title=2021 End Of Season Awards|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7656|access-date=2022-02-02|website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

Following the retirement of [[Michael Shenton]], McShane was named as the new Castleford captain in January 2022.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Bazeley|first=Marc|date=2022-01-13|title=Paul McShane: England hooker named Castleford Tigers captain ahead of 2022 Super League season|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12514433/paul-mcshane-england-hooker-named-castleford-tigers-captain-ahead-of-2022-super-league-season|access-date=2022-02-02|website=Sky Sports}}</ref> He made 28 appearances throughout the season, missing just one game through suspension.<ref name="RLRKC" />

In the [[2023 Castleford Tigers season|2023 season]], McShane scored a try against [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] on 6 April in a key victory against Castleford's relegation rivals.<ref>{{Cite web |date=6 April 2023 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 16–4 Wakefield Trinity – Hosts claim derby victory over winless visitors |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65188795 |access-date=24 March 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He made 14 appearances in total, also kicking eight goals.<ref name="RLRKC" /> On 9 June, during the defeat against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]], he sustained a fractured forearm which would force him to miss the remaining months of the season.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=13 June 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers suffer major blow with Paul McShane timeframe revealed plus update on alternative options, including Jacques O'Neill |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-suffer-major-blow-with-paul-mcshane-timeframe-revealed-plus-update-on-alternative-options-including-jacques-oneill |access-date=24 March 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

McShane was awarded a testimonial year for the [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=5 June 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers captain Paul McShane awarded testimonial for 2024 |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-captain-paul-mcshane-awarded-testimonial-for-2024/ |access-date=24 March 2024 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> Castleford faced the [[Huddersfield Giants]] for his [[testimonial match]] on 4 February.<ref>{{Cite web |date=4 February 2024 |title=Tigers win in McShane testimonial! |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8871 |access-date=24 March 2024 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Cook |first=Sam |date=4 February 2024 |title=Ex-Hull KR man bags brace as Castleford Tigers round off pre-season with a win |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/ex-hull-kr-man-bags-brace-as-castleford-tigers-round-off-pre-season-with-a-win-87848/ |access-date=24 March 2024 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref> Shortly before the first round, McShane suffered a hamstring injury which ruled him out for the opening six weeks.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=14 February 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers rocked with Paul McShane set for significant injury layoff |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-paul-mcshane-injury-28630966 |access-date=24 March 2024 |website=Yorkshire Live}}</ref> After nine months on the sidelines, McShane made his competitive return on 23 March against [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] in the [[Challenge Cup]] and marked the occasion by scoring a try and two goals.<ref>{{Cite web |date=23 March 2024 |title=Challenge Cup: Batley Bulldogs 14–28 Castleford Tigers: Paul McShane stars as Super League side progress |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68616745 |access-date=24 March 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In late May, complications arose regarding his previous arm injury and he underwent surgery to remove the [[orthopedic plate]]s.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=13 June 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers' Paul McShane facing 'open-ended' time off with operation outcome key |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-paul-mcshane-operation-29347937 |access-date=9 August 2024 |website=Yorkshire Live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=2 July 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers boss Craig Lingard on Paul McShane injury, George Lawler comeback and recruitment struggles |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-boss-craig-lingard-on-paul-mcshane-injury-george-lawler-comeback-and-recruitment-struggles-4687746 |access-date=9 August 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> On 5 September, it was announced that McShane would retire from playing full-time at the end of the season,<ref>{{Cite web |date=6 November 2024 |title=Cas captain and ex-Man of Steel McShane to retire |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2xvlkpygeo |access-date=6 November 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> and he received a guard of honour at his final home game.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=8 September 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers coach's classy words to retiring club legend following emotional final home appearance |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-coachs-classy-words-to-retiring-club-legend-following-emotional-final-home-appearance |access-date=6 November 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=7 September 2024 |title=Craig Lingard frustrated after Castleford Tigers fail to do Paul McShane justice |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/craig-lingard-frustrated-after-castleford-tigers-fail-to-do-paul-mcshane-justice-4773008 |access-date=6 November 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref>

===York Knights===
On 6 November 2024, it was announced that McShane had signed for the [[York Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=6 November 2024 |title=Former Super League Man of Steel agrees deal to join Championship club |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-super-league-man-of-steel-agrees-deal-to-join-championship-club |access-date=6 November 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=6 November 2024 |title=Ex-Castleford Tigers captain Paul McShane signs for new club |url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-castleford-tigers-captain-paul-mcshane-signs-for-new-club/ |access-date=6 November 2024 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> In addition to his playing duties, he would also join the coaching staff and work alongside [[Mark Applegarth]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=6 November 2024 |title='Something I've wanted to do for a long time': Paul McShane joins York Knights in dual role |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/something-ive-wanted-to-do-for-a-long-time-paul-mcshane-joins-york-knights-in-dual-role-4855117 |access-date=6 November 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> On 7 June 2025, he played in York's [[2025 RFL 1895 Cup#Final|1895 Cup final]] victory over [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cm26dp2l3vvo |title=York pip Featherstone in extra time to win 1895 Cup |work=BBC Sport |date=7 June 2025 |access-date=8 June 2025}}</ref>

==International career==
McShane was first named in England's elite performance squad in May 2017.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Bower|first=Aaron|date=2017-06-02|title=Paul McShane best hooker in Super League after England call, says Daryl Powell|url=https://www.totalrl.com/paul-mcshane-best-hooker-super-league-england-call-says-daryl-powell/|access-date=2022-02-02|website=TotalRL}}</ref>

McShane was named in [[Shaun Wane]]'s first England squad in March 2020, in preparation for the end-of-season [[The Ashes (rugby league)|Ashes]] series.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-03-03|title=England head coach Shaun Wane names first squad ahead of end-of-year Ashes series|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11948538/england-head-coach-shaun-wane-names-first-squad-ahead-of-end-of-year-ashes-series|access-date=2022-02-02|website=Sky Sports}}</ref> However, the series was later cancelled due to the [[COVID-19 pandemic]].

McShane earned his first senior England cap against the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]] on 25 June 2021 at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-06-26|title=England 24–26 All Stars: Shaun Wane makes losing start with England|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/england-vs-combined-nations-all-stars/report/55444|access-date=2022-02-03|website=Sky Sports}}</ref> He represented England in their 30–10 victory over [[France national rugby league team|France]] at the [[Stade Gilbert Brutus]] on 23 October 2021.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Hafez|first=Shamoon|date=2021-10-23|title=France 10–30 England: Bateman, Farrell, Davies, Makinson with tries|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/59022672|access-date=2022-02-03|website=BBC Sport}}</ref>
